name: Kentik Network Observability API
what_it_does: Enterprise network observability platform with REST and gRPC APIs for NetFlow/sFlow analysis, BGP monitoring, DDoS detection, cloud network visibility, and network traffic analytics at carrier and enterprise scale.
use_cases: Querying network traffic flow data for capacity planning and anomaly detection agents
DDoS attack detection and mitigation trigger automation via Alerts API
BGP routing analysis and Internet peering optimization for network operations
Cloud network cost attribution by analyzing traffic flow data between cloud services
Network security investigation via SQL-like query API for flow data exploration
not_for: Small networks — Kentik targets carrier, CDN, and large enterprise environments
Simple website uptime monitoring (use Pingdom/UptimeRobot instead)
best_when: An agent analyzes network traffic at scale for a carrier, CDN, or large enterprise needing flow-level visibility, BGP intelligence, and DDoS mitigation automation.
avoid_when: You don't send flow data — Kentik requires NetFlow/sFlow/IPFIX data from your network devices.

auth.notes: API key from Kentik portal with email header for authentication. User email + API key required as X-CH-Auth-Email and X-CH-Auth-API-Token headers. gRPC uses same credentials. Per-user key management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing: typically $100k+/year based on flow volume
pricing.notes: Kentik is enterprise network intelligence — contact sales. Pricing based on flow data volume (FPS — flows per second). Targets telcos, CDNs, and large enterprises.

performance.rate_limits: 10 concurrent queries per account; query timeout configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer portal with REST and gRPC documentation. SQL-like query language for flow analysis. Error responses are JSON. gRPC streaming for real-time data access. Go SDK and Python SDK available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Query operations are read-only and idempotent. Alert policy creation may produce duplicates — check before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Dual auth headers required: email + API key in X-CH-Auth-Email and X-CH-Auth-API-Token
Query language is Kentik-specific SQL variant — learning curve for new users
Flow data has ingestion delay (seconds to minutes) — not real-time for sub-second analysis
gRPC and REST APIs have different feature coverage — some features only in one

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II and ISO 27001. TLS enforced for all API communication. Dual-header auth (email + API key) is non-standard but functional. Network flow data is sensitive — encrypted at rest and in transit.
